name: the-warmth-of-other-suns
description: Isabel Wilkerson's 'The Warmth of Other Suns: The Epic Story of America's Great Migration' — the definitive account of 6 million African Americans who fled the Jim Crow South between 1915 and 1970, told through three unforgettable personal stories.
useCase: Understand the Great Migration, the Jim Crow South, the journeys of migrants, and how this mass movement reshaped American cities, culture, and politics.
knownRisks: Lengthy and detailed — the skill covers key narratives but cannot capture every story
Contains frank depictions of racial violence, lynching, and discrimination
Primarily focused on African American migration — does not cover other migrations in the same period
